response.justification: While this candidate brings strong software engineering fundamentals and 14+ years of experience, this is a hardware-focused Principal Performance Architect role requiring SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design expertise. The candidate's background is entirely in software development (web, mobile, embedded), with no demonstrated experience in hardware architecture, accelerator design, deep learning workload optimization at the hardware level, or performance modeling of silicon. A successful candidate would need deep expertise in hardware performance analysis and AI accelerator optimization—a fundamentally different domain.
strengths: 14+ years of professional software engineering with demonstrated ability to optimize for performance (low-latency systems, async I/O, caching)
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, code quality focus) that could transfer to hardware verification and modeling
Currently working on AI-agent infrastructure and tooling, showing engagement with AI/ML technology ecosystem
concerns: Zero background in hardware architecture, SoC design, or IP modeling—all core requirements for this principal-level role
No demonstrated expertise in GPU architecture, AI accelerators, or hardware-software co-design, which are essential for the position
Career is entirely software-focused; lacks the systems engineering and hardware performance analysis background required for deep learning accelerator optimization
